attributes_descriptionhtml: Hi, I’m Beamer. Four other kittens and I were rescued by Animal Control and luckily came to the SBACC, where we were safe, warm, and well-fed while waiting for our forever homes. I was the smallest of the group and was more scared and shy. I’ve been at the shelter for several years now, and I have a lot of fun zooming around the room, playing, and spending time with my roommates.  I am still nervous around people, and I don’t allow pets or being picked up, but I LOVE other cats. I would need a patient family willing to let me be me, and not expect me to become a people-friendly cat. I would definitely have to have another feline in my home.  My best friends at SBACC are Osaka, Oshkosh, Abbie, and Angelique.  We’re all shy and hang out together, and I would love to go home with one of them.  I’m with Abbie and Oshkosh in one of the pics.

attributes_descriptionhtml: Hi, I’m Twinkle.  I was rescued from the Garden Center at the Seal Beach Target along with my kittens, who were just three days old.  It was just a couple of weeks before Christmas of 2016, and there were no foster homes for us, but I got to raise my kittens in the comfort and safety of our very own room at SBACC.  When my kittens learned to eat on their own, they went to a foster home until they were big enough and ready for their forever homes. They were all adopted. I’m still very shy with people, and I don’t welcome pets or being picked up.  I do love other cats, and Val and I, another shy cat, and I have become best friends.   My dream is to find a forever home with Val with a family who understands shy cats and would give us lots of time to adjust and get comfortable.  That’s Val with me in one of the pics.

attributes_descriptionhtml: Hi, I’m Osaka. I was rescued from Leisure World along with my two lookalike brothers, Oshkosh and Oyster, when we were about four months old. Since we were older kittens and had spent more time on our own, we were pretty scared of being around people. I have come a long way, but I am still pretty shy, I don’t welcome pets or being picked up. I’d love to go home with Oshkosh or another kitten or cat. That's him with me in one pic.

attributes_descriptionhtml:
Hi, I’m Bart. I was named Bartleby back in 2010 when I arrived at SBACC at just six weeks old. I was adopted but sadly returned because somewhere along the way, I developed the habit of not using the litterbox consistently. I’ve been checked out and there aren’t any medical reasons for my behavior, it seems to have become a habit.  I am a mellow, friendly boy, I enjoy attention and pets and I love to “make bread”. I’m hoping there’s someone who has the right setup or a way to try and work with my behavior because I love people and would really like to have a home again.

My adoption fee has been paid by a generous donor!

attributes_descriptionhtml:
I want to introduce Luca and Jasper to you!  These 2 sweet boys were adopted from us as kittens in 2018 and due to some health issues with their mama (who loves and adores them very much) the decision was made for the boys to come back to our rescue and find a new forever home. Sometimes this is what’s best for all involved and we are supportive of this decision, please no negative comments on this. It was the best decision for all parties. 

 

I can absolutely say, both boys are very loving and sweet once they get to know you.  The boys are 5 years old now. 

 

Jasper has a medical condition known as cystitis, this is a life long condition. It can flare up when he gets stressed.  He takes a pill everyday, the cost is $4.50 every 2 months from a Walmart pharmacy prescription. And he is on a CD prescription diet to help control his condition.  Both boys eat the same CD food and they get dry food and wet food daily.

 

Luca has something that is pretty rare for cats, he can sometimes faint after he has thrown up.  There is no treatment needed. He just needs to be watched and given licky cat treats to help him come back around. 

 

Here are the bios their mama wrote about them to help them find their new home. 

 

Luca is the biggest lovebug you will ever meet. Luca can be a little shy at first but once he trusts you all he wants is to snuggle with you and sit on your lap while you watch tv or read. If you happen to forget to pet him well, don't worry about it, he will gently tap you with a paw or his face to let you know he's there. He also enjoys laying on your chest when its time for bed, he plops right down and purrs away, he especially loves getting belly rubs like this! Luca loves to be brushed and will come running if he hears you brushing your own hair. Some of his favorite toys are springs, which he fetches, and laser toys! Luca is very agile and loves running up the cat tree and up and down his cat wall and down the hallways. He also very much loves snuggling with his brother Jasper and you can occasionally find him walking around squeaking looking for Jasper to play! Luca is the best friend you could ever ask for! 

 

Jasper is a character, lovingly nicknamed Lil' Squeaks. If you've ever wanted a cat you can have a conversation with then look no further! Jasper has opinions about everything and will happily tell you all about his busy day of napping when you get home from work! Jasper absolutely loves a good wand toy, and if he thinks you aren't looking he might be found playing with springs or little ball toys. Jasper loves to sleep on your pillow next to you at night, he will squeak at you for a head scratch if you happen to wake up in the night. His favorite thing is getting his face washed by his brother Luca, when this happens it looks like he is in absolute heaven! Jasper loves belly rubs and will often grab your arm so he can lick you while you rub his tummy. 

 

If you are interesting in meeting this special pair of boys. Please contact us at catcoverescue@gmail.com for more information.
attributes_searchstring: Luca Sinbad Orange and white Orange and White Male  Best Friends Orange and white Cats Domestic Short Hair (short coat)s

attributes_descriptionhtml:
Puzzle

 

I know, I know. It’s just the look I have. Puzzled. So that’s my name Puzzle.  But it’s okay, because I’m so much happier indoors with my brother, Whisp, than out there in the cold alleys. I’ll take the name Puzzle if it means I get to eat snacks and sleep in that amazing cat hammock...Wait...Hold on...did somebody say snacks?  I’m sure I heard a snack bag shaking just now. Oh wait, that was me. Sorry. I get a little side tracked when someone brings up snacks. Right, ok, so about me...well, my best friend is my brother, Whisp. We go everywhere together, so we gotta go into a happy home together. We are both pretty shy, and loud noises kinda scare me still.  I haven’t fully been able to shake of the life I lived before the warmth of the indoors,  but I know that when that perfect comes for me and my brother, that person with have the love and patience needed to help us adjust and feel safe. We require a hammock to help in the transition, lots of toys...especially the laser pointer. I mean it’s mandatory. Oh, and snacks, I have a favorite actually...licky treats. We just turned 1 year old this month of April 2019, and I was so happy to get licky treats! They are like gogurts for cats! Seriously. They are the best. I enjoy a human bed on occasion and to be next to human legs because hey are warm and...I’m hearing that snack bag again. Do you hear that? You should come over hear and have a snack with me. BYOS! See you soon!
attributes_searchstring: Puzzle  Tabby and white Brown Tabby Female Medium Petsmart - Long Beach Tabby and white Cats Domestic Short Hair (short coat)s

attributes_descriptionhtml:
Whisp

 

I’m sleek, lean, and handsome. I’ve got this beautiful undercoat of white beneath my black exterior which makes me not just your average tuxedo kitty. Whisps of inconspicuous white swirls within the black of me...hmmmm I thunk I just figured out why they call me Whisp! That’s Cool. So, I am on the shier end of the spectrum, but I’m a ham for belly rubs especially if you catch me while I’m napping. My sister Puzzle is my bestie, so we come as a pair. We grew up on the rough streets of Long Beach alleys until our foster mom caught us and loved us enough for us to believe humans can be kind. We were pretty scared out there, so it takes us a while to adjust to new surroundings. We will do best in a home without kids or dogs or other cats mainly because we want all of your attention, and we will be more able to come out and shine if it’s just the two of us taking over your bed. Now days where spend most of our time together in our special hammock we got as a present. We LOVE THAT THING! We also love the laser pointer, my sister is a laser pointer hog, but the thing is so elusive that she can’t catch it either! We just had our first birthday, we were so excited to get birthday snacks ( snacks are my absolute favorite thing ever.) Hard to believe I was born in April of 2018 and it’s already 2019. Time sure does fly. I can’t wait for time to go by in a loving forever home with my sister...that sounds like a dream come true  ❤️ . I wonder who will come to love us...could it be you?
attributes_searchstring: Whisp   Black and White Male  Petsmart - Long Beach  Cats Domestic Short Hair / Mixed (short coat)s

attributes_descriptionhtml: Hi, I’m Val.  I was about four months old and all alone when I was rescued from the street.  I was very, very scared, and after coming to SBACC, I went to a foster home so I could get used to people and other cats.  I spent about six weeks in foster care and then came back to SBACC.  I’ve adjusted to my room and roommates at SBACC, but I still don’t welcome pets or being picked up.  Twinkle, another shy cat, and I have become best friends, and it would be wonderful if we could find a home together.  It will need to be a quiet forever home and with a family who understands shy cats and will have lots of patience and give us plenty of time to adjust and feel comfortable.  That’s Twinkle and me in one of the pics.

attributes_descriptionhtml: Hi, I’m Angelique. My three littermates and I were rescued from the street in Seal Beach when we were about two months old.  We were all very scared as we hadn’t been around people before. At first, we were considered feral, but we were sent to a foster home and calmed down quite a bit.  My foster home had other kittens, big cats, and dogs. I loved the big cats and was fine with the kittens, but the dogs scared me. Two of our sibs were adopted by an SBACC volunteer.  I’m a shy girl who gets scared easily, and I don’t welcome pets or being picked up. I’m really quite content at the shelter with my sister Abbie and several other cats who don’t welcome the human touch.

attributes_descriptionhtml: Hi, I’m Ben.  I originally came to SBACC with my brother, Joe, when we were just four weeks old.  We went to a foster home where we became friendly, active kittens. Our names were Frederick and Foxy back then. When we were adopted our names were changed to Ben and Joe and we both answer to those names now. We had a home for a year and a half but then were returned to SBACC  because of a serious illness in one of the children in our family.  We are both friendly and affectionate cats and were adopted together again.  Sadly, it didn’t work out because one of us developed the habit of sometimes peeing out of the litter box.  Was it me or was it Joe or was it both of us, no one was sure.  After we came back to SBACC again, it was noticed that the two of us didn’t get along all that well, so we were put in different rooms where our habits could be observed.  My brother was using the box consistently and he found a new home.  I continued with my inappropriate behavior, not all of the time, but some of the time.  I think I need to be an only cat and need a new family who will take a chance on me.  In the right home, my inappropriate behavior might stop.  If it doesn’t, SBACC  will always take me back.

attributes_descriptionhtml:
Ghost was a community cat cared for by a lovely couple who could not take him in. He was found covered in tar one day, and they reached out for help - how could we say no? - and that's the day he became a Jellicle Cat.   He is estimated to have been born January 2019

He's a little shy at first but will warm up once you earn his trust. He's sometimes chatty, sometimes serious. He likes other kitties well enough but may do ok on his own in the right home. Probably not a good kid cat, as he a little shy at times.

Ghost loves loves loves to lay on your feet while you're inert, and keep you from being productive. So if you are looking for your perfect quarantine companion, it's ghost!

attributes_descriptiontext:
* In Foster Care * Meet & Greets by appointment. * Please submit an application online and we’ll call to schedule! * 
Sonny is the kind of cat who quietly becomes your shadow. He’ll follow you from room to room with soft little chirps, happy just to be near you. Settle onto the couch and he’ll be right there beside you, purring like it’s his full-time job.
At 10 years young, Sonny has perfected the art of companionship. He’s mellow, affectionate, and wonderfully easy to live with. He enjoys playtime in short bursts, appreciates a cozy nap, and truly thrives on simple, steady connection.
Sonny is FIV positive, which simply means his immune system needs a little extra care. FIV+ cats can live long, happy lives indoors, and Sonny has done this beautifully. He also has kidney disease that has remained stable on a prescription diet alone. He feels great, loves his special food, and continues to enjoy each day to the fullest.
What Sonny wants most is a calm, loving home where he can be someone’s steady companion. If you’re looking for a loyal, gentle presence who will quietly fill your home with warmth, Sonny may be exactly who you’ve been waiting for
